title: Cutting and Slicing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice materials, such as glass, stone, cork, rubber, tobacco, food, paper, or insulating material.

tasks.text: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice materials, such as glass, stone, cork, rubber, tobacco, food, paper, or insulating material.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review work orders, blueprints, specifications, or job samples to determine components, settings, and adjustments for cutting and slicing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Examine, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices, such as rulers, micrometers, or scales.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Press buttons, pull levers, or depress pedals to start and operate cutting and slicing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start machines to verify setups, and make any necessary adjustments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Feed stock into cutting machines, onto conveyors, or under cutting blades, by threading, guiding, pushing, or turning handwheels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor operation of cutting or slicing machines to detect malfunctions or to determine whether supplies need replenishment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Stack and sort cut material for packaging, further processing, or shipping, according to types and sizes of material.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Adjust machine controls to alter position, alignment, speed, or pressure.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove completed materials or products from cutting or slicing machines, and stack or store them for additional processing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain production records, such as quantities, types, and dimensions of materials produced.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Remove defective or substandard materials from machines, and readjust machine components so that products meet standards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position stock along cutting lines, or against stops on beds of scoring or cutting machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Move stock or scrap to and from machines manually, or by using carts, handtrucks, or lift trucks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select and install machine components, such as cutting blades, rollers, and templates, according to specifications, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean and lubricate cutting machines, conveyors, blades, saws, or knives, using steam hoses, scrapers, brushes, or oil cans.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mark cutting lines or identifying information on stock, using marking pencils, rulers, or scribes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Type instructions on computer keyboards, push buttons to activate computer programs, or manually set cutting guides, clamps, and knives.
tasks.rationale: Software coding, programming, or application development task highly susceptible to automated generation.

tasks.text: Change or replace saw blades, cables, cutter heads, and grinding wheels, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position width gauge blocks between blades, and level blades and insert wedges into frames to secure blades to frames.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct workers on cutting teams.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Sharpen cutting blades, knives, or saws, using files, bench grinders, or honing stones.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn cranks or press buttons to activate winches that move cars under sawing cables or saw frames.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Tighten pulleys or add abrasives to maintain cutting speeds.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut stock manually to prepare for machine cutting, using tools such as knives, cleavers, handsaws, or hammers and chisels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
